items.title: US and Iran exchange strikes for the third day
items.description: A serious escalation is occurring in the fighting between the US and Iran. Overnight and into this morning, the American military targeted sites across the Islamic Republic – while Iran fired ballistic missiles at US airbases in Jordan and Bahrain. There has been an escalation in the war of words too: President Donald Trump vowed to reinstate an American blockade of Iranian ports and charge ships for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz. Details and analysis by Scott Lucas, professor of US and international politics at the Clinton Institute of University College Dublin.

The semifinal between France and Spain will see two of the tournament's best teams clash on Tuesday, with French stars such as Kylian Mbappé and Ousmane Dembélé contending with a Spanish defence that has only conceded one goal all tournament. Fans of both Les Bleus and La Roja are hopeful they can just pip the other to a spot in Sunday's final. Story by Camille Corcoran.

items.title: Paramount's Warner takeover challenged by 12 US states
items.description: California is leading the legal charge against one of the world's biggest media mergers ever. Twelve US states have filed a lawsuit seeking to block Paramount Skydance from acquiring Warner Brothers Discovery – saying the deal would create a company with too much market power and end up harming consumers and workers. The legal challenge is being seen in part as a political proxy battle – with Democratic state officials seeking to rein in what many see as unchecked crony capitalism and corruption based on business leaders' proximity to President Donald Trump. Details by Bryan Quinn.

items.title: Spain's 'Formidable Opponent': Rajoy credits France's football success to having 'no French players'
items.description: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Dr Leon Moosavi, Sociologist of race and religion, and Reader in the Department of Sociology, Social Policy and Criminology at the University of Liverpool. Racism directed at the French national football team is often framed as a problem of sport, yet Dr. Moosavi argues that football merely provides a highly visible stage upon which much deeper political and social conflicts play out. Rather than viewing racist abuse against players such as Kylian Mbappé as isolated incidents or the actions of a few extremists, our guest describes these attacks within wider debates over national identity, immigration, belonging, and the normalization of extremist rhetoric and exclusionary political discourse across many Western democracies.

items.title: 26-year-old shot and killed by ICE agent in Maine sparks demonstrations
items.description: US ICE agents shot and killed a 26-year-old Colombian man in Maine early Monday morning. Department of Homeland Security secretary Markwayne Mullin first said that the man was a target of an ICE investigation and that he had attempted to "weaponise" his vehicle. Later the story changed – it was confirmed that the man was not the target of the investigation, and DHS described his car as "attempting to flee the scene". Simon Moritz sheds light on the situation that has sparked anti-ICE demonstrations in the area.

items.title: The U.S.-Iran ceasefire grows more distant. And, Congress faces a consequential week
items.description: The U.S. and Iran exchanged fire over the weekend amid tensions over the Strait of Hormuz. And, Congress returns from recess facing tight deadlines.

items.title: Israeli strike on police post in north Gaza kills seven, officials say
items.description: A senior officer in the Hamas-run police force was among those killed in the strike, which Israel's military says targeted "terrorists".

items.title: Argentina's Lionel Scaloni says England World Cup semi 'just a football game'
items.description: "It is a football game and we will be playing against a very tough opponent. This is a football game and that's all," declares Argentina boss. Leer más

items.title: U.S. strikes Iran for third day, will reimpose blockade
items.description: President Trump said a deal with the Islamic Republic was still possible even as attacks were carried out, with Iran striking two ships in the strategic waterway and killing one crew member

items.title: World Cup stickers traded en masse in Mexico City as part of world record attempt
items.description: Thousands of people gathered Sunday in Mexico City's Zócalo to swap 2026 World Cup soccer stickers as part of a Guinness World Record attempt

items.title: Nasa's Anil Menon, two Russians blast off for 8-month International Space Station mission
items.description: Mr. Menon will "conduct scientific research and technology demonstrations aimed at advancing human space exploration and benefiting life on Earth," according to NASA

items.title: China, Russia conclude joint naval drills in Yellow Sea
items.description: The ships' crews practiced joint maneuvering, anti-submarine missions, combat against enemy surface ships, and rescue operations, Russian Defence Ministry said

items.title: Hungary: Lawmakers pass law to oust Orban ally president
items.description: The Hungarian parliament continued its attempts to leave the Viktor Orban era behind the country by moving to oust President Tamas Sulyok.

items.title: World Cup: Balogun red and expansion keep FIFA in spotlight
items.description: The fallout from Folarin Balogun's red card reversal continues as reports suggest the chairman of FIFA’s disciplinary committee made the decision unilaterally. Meanwhile, the World Cup could be set to expand once more.

items.title: India summons Iranian diplomat over missile killing of seafarer
items.description: India summoned Iran's deputy ambassador after an Indian crew member was killed in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: People flee Bangkok bar as huge flames shoot out from doorway
items.description: At least 28 people have died and 25 are critically injured after a fire tore through a bar in a popular Bangkok district.

items.title: India supports UN membership for Palestine, says MEA
items.description: India’s position on Palestine was apprised by Sripriya Ranganathan, a secretary at the External Affairs Ministry, during a ministerial meeting of the Palestine Donor Group, held in Brussels July 13

items.title: How a Jewish heir is taking on Germany's far-right AfD
items.description: The AfD is using the iconic GDR-era Simson moped in its campaign. Now, a Jewish heir to the founding family is fighting to protect the company's legacy.

items.title: US President Trump drops 20% Strait of Hormuz fee plan
items.description: US President Donald Trump says he has abandoned his proposed 20% fee on cargo transiting the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Chirag Veer Singh Sarao becomes first Sikh American to join U.S. Air Force Academy
items.description: On April 4, Mr. Sarao was appointed to the U.S. Air Force Academy and received the ‘religious accommodation’ on June 20 to serve with his articles of faith and remain grounded in his core identity said, officials

items.title: Argentina to repay US$4 billion, defying critics who doubted tack
items.description: Argentina is set to make a major payment on its dollar bonds this week, pulling off a feat few investors thought possible. Leer más

items.title: Japanese sake brewery to make foray into Indian market
items.description: Takahashi Shouten, based in the city of Yame in Fukuoka Prefecture, launched a subsidiary in India in February.

items.title: Casino Trends: Regulations and what players should know
items.description: How regulation, mobile design, bonus transparency, and responsible gaming tools are changing the online casino market in 2026 based on data from review platforms like Slotozilla. The post Casino Trends: Regulations and what players should know appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Bangkok bar fire death toll rises to 30, 24 people remain in critical condition
items.description: At least 30 people have now been confirmed dead in a blaze that engulfed a bar in northern Bangkok in the early hours of Monday morning. 27 victims have been formally identified, with authorities still working to confirm the identities of the remaining three. Meanwhile, forensic investigators are seeking to establish the cause of the blaze, with the bar agreeing to comply with an investigation into alleged negligence. Story by Camille Corcoran.

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: De Beers to pause work at S.Africa's largest diamond mine
items.description: De Beers announced Monday it will pause production at South Africa's largest diamond mine for two years to reduce costs while trading conditions remained tough

items.title: Sheikh Hasina will land in jail if she returns: Bangladesh minister
items.description: State Minister for Foreign Affairs Shama Obaed Islam said the process to bring Sheikh Hasina back began during the previous interim regime and is now being pursued by the Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government

items.title: Australian police reveal unseen photos 25 years after British backpacker murder
items.description: Police hope the images could jog memories that will help them find the body of Peter Falconio.

items.title: Buffett severs donations to Gates Foundation following Epstein revelations
items.description: The American investor will end his longtime donations to the Microsoft co-founder's non-profit.

items.title: Iran MPs present bill on Strait of Hormuz: Security Committee Chief
items.description: U.S.-Iran fighting reignited last week over disagreements on how the vital strait would be managed

items.title: Launceston General Hospital experiencing 'impacted' capacity due to high demand
items.description: Tasmania's top health official says the Launceston General Hospital is experiencing "impacted" capacity and is urging patients to only attend if it is a medical emergency.

items.title: Victorian teachers set to strike again following deadlocked negotiations
items.description: A fresh statewide strike by Victorian public school teachers will he held next week, after negotiations with the Victorian government reached an impasse.

items.title: Giant petrel found on Noosa beach tests negative for bird flu
items.description: The giant petrel was taken to RSPCA Queensland's Wildlife Rehabilitation Centre where it underwent testing by Biosecurity Queensland as part of its surveillance program.

items.title: One Indian sailor killed, six wounded as Iranian missiles strike oil tankers in Strait of Hormuz
items.description: Missiles hit the Mombasa and Al Bahiyah in ​Omani territorial waters; fires damaged both tankers but crews brought them under control

items.title: Aussie investors fear millions in losses as alleged fake bond scheme unravels
items.description: The financial watchdog is seeking to shut down an investment company accused of taking $17 million from 80 victims, including by promoting fake bonds.

items.title: Scaloni admits Argentina 'suffered' in Switzerland win
items.description: Argentina coach says his side "suffered" in their 3-1 extra-time victory over Switzerland. Leer más

items.title: Zelenskyy joins Macron and European leaders in Paris for Bastille Day
items.description: Volodymyr Zelenskyy joins Emmanuel Macron and European leaders at a Ukraine-focused Bastille Day parade in Paris showcasing military strength.

items.title: UK police arrest murder suspect in Ann Widdecombe's death
items.description: Police in southwest England say they have arrested a 26-year-old man on suspicion of murdering politician Ann Widdecombe. Based on initial inquiries, they were not treating the case as terrorism or politically motivated.

items.title: U.S., Iran trade claims over Strait of Hormuz as tensions escalate
items.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard rejected that claim, declaring the strait to be Iranian territory and warning that it would not tolerate what it called illegal U.S. interference.

items.title: Jaishankar discusses global developments, including West Asia, Ukraine with UN Chief Guterres
items.description: Mr. Jaishankar launched India’s official campaign for a non-permanent seat at the UN Security Council for 2028-29 at an event at the world body’s headquarters in the United Nations that was attended by UN ambassadors, diplomats and officials

items.title: Milei urges governors to renew commitment to 'May Pact' in speech
items.description: President says Argentina is entering a "Second Independence" as he outlines new reform agenda and praises his "cabinet of patriots." Leer más

items.title: Sam Neill, known for 'Jurassic Park' and 'The Piano,' dies at 78, his family says
items.description: In 2023, Neill disclosed he had been diagnosed with angioimmunoblastic T-cell lymphoma, a rare type of non-Hodgkin lymphoma. Neill died on Monday in Sydney, according to a statement posted to the actor's social media page.

items.title: U.S. announces 20% toll on ships using Hormuz; what Trump’s Iranian blockade means
items.description: Donald Trump said the United States would be “reimbursed” through a levy amounting to 20 per cent of the value of cargo passing through the waterway

items.title: Argentina’s political parties look for ways to avoid extremes
items.description: With next year’s election set to offer a referendum on President Javier Milei’s reformist agenda, some influential business leaders and opposition figures are discussing the possibility that violent shifts of policy are coming to an end – regardless of who prevails in the 2027 vote. items.title: Ukraine targets another 11 Russian ships in Sea of Azov
items.description: Ukraine said it struck tankers and cargo vessels in the Sea of Azov, in the latest strikes on Russian commercial shipping. Moscow's Agriculture Ministry said "alternative shipping routes" are being sought.

items.title: German minister warns against unacceptable delays to 10 billion euro satellite project
‘Piracy’: Will Trump’s 20 percent Hormuz toll find takers?
items.description: Trump says the US will start charging a cargo fee for ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Shock in South African football: World Cup star Jayden Adams dies at 25
items.description: South African football is in mourning after World Cup player Jayden Adams was found dead at a property in Cape Town. Police have opened an inquest, while authorities await autopsy results. Tributes poured in as the football community remembered the 25-year-old midfielder.

items.title: Argentina v England: Battles all over the pitch
items.description: Wednesday's World Cup semi-final between Argentina and England in Atlanta promises to follow in the footsteps of the fiery encounters that have defined this rivalry, with compelling individual and tactical battles across the pitch. Leer más

items.title: US risks falling into ‘escalation trap’ if it deploys troops in Iran
items.description: Analyst Alex Alfirraz Scheers says the US risks falling into an escalation trap if it launches a ground assault in Iran.

items.title: 'I'm ruling it out': Taylor categorically kills off One Nation coalition talk
items.description: Coalition leader Angus Taylor has renewed his criticism of Pauline Hanson's party and categorically ruled out any alliance with them.

items.title: Mundial trumps Trump
items.description: ‘The Donald’ had bullied obsequious FIFA president Gianni Infantino into nixing a red card for the home team’s top scorer – an obscene subordination of the beautiful game to power politics. Leer más

items.title: French Embassy in Argentina slams Mendoza official's ‘racist’ remarks
items.description: Hebe Casado, Mendoza's right-wing deputy governor, defends herself after being barred from bilateral meetings with the French Embassy; Official called France's football team "ill-mannered African team" following World Cup win over Paraguay, comments French Ambassador Romain Nadal says were unambiguously racist. Leer más

items.title: Milei’s allied governors open door to suspension of PASO primaries
items.description: Government is seeking support to advance with its electoral reform push ahead of next voting cycle; Senate to have final say on potential suspension of compulsory primaries. Leer más

items.title: Argentina cling to Messi in bid to break Europe’s World Cup grip
items.description: Reigning champions are the only non-European side left in the tournament and must overcome Switzerland to keep Lionel Messi’s dream alive. Leer más

items.title: Germany news: Neuschwanstein killer fights extradition to US
items.description: A man who murdered a woman at Neuschwanstein Castle says he fears execution in the US. Germany has been hit by another heat wave, though no records are likely to be broken this time, forecasters say.

items.title: Poland could cut arms aid to Ukraine as WWII spat continues
items.description: As Poland observes a day of remembrance for the victims of a Ukrainian nationalist military unit, there are increasing signs that it could cut off its neighbor from military aid, even as the war against Russia continues.

items.title: Ebola: Workers at Congolese treatment center strike over unpaid salaries
items.description: The striking staff includes epidemiologists, case investigators, drivers and gravediggers who say they have not been paid by the Congolese authorities

items.title: Strait of Hormuz remains a fault line as Iran and US drift back into war
items.description: Control of the waterway has become a key point of contention between the US and Iran, as a ceasefire deal falters.

items.title: France opens terror probe after gun found in Jewish suburb
items.description: Police said they found a "military-grade weapon" during the search of a suspicious car parked in a Paris neighborhood with a large Jewish population.

items.title: Perpetuals.com ends acquisition talks with Trump-linked crypto firm
England, Argentina to renew bitter rivalry in World Cup semi-final
items.description: The Albiceleste and their talisman Lionel Messi must defeat England in Atlanta to keep alive their hopes of becoming the first team to win back-to-back World Cups since Brazil in 1962. Leer más
